journal_entry.description: Deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Atlas has received $5,000 cash but has not yet performed the moving services. The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue/deferred revenue) because Atlas has an obligation to either perform the service or refund the money. The asset increases (cash in chequing account) and the liability increases (unearned revenue). No revenue is recognized at this point because the performance obligation (providing moving services) will be satisfied in one month. The tuples correctly identify this as one asset increase (debit) and one liability increase (credit). No tax is recorded on the deposit because HST/GST is recognized when revenue is earned, not when deposits are received.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas debits its bank/chequing account (asset increase). The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) which is credited, but we only classify the debit side here.

debit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas debits its bank/chequing account (asset increase). The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) which is credited, but we only classify the debit side here.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, which is NOT a credit-side event (assets are debited when increased). Actually, the deposit creates an obligation to perform services = liability increase (unearned revenue/customer deposit), and (2) cash is received = asset increase on the debit side. On the CREDIT side: the $5,000 deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue) because Atlas has not yet performed the moving services = liability increase (1 line).

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, which is NOT a credit-side event (assets are debited when increased). Actually, the deposit creates an obligation to perform services = liability increase (unearned revenue/customer deposit), and (2) cash is received = asset increase on the debit side. On the CREDIT side: the $5,000 deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue) because Atlas has not yet performed the moving services = liability increase (1 line).

output.reason: Entry correctly records deposit for future services under IFRS 15. Debit to Chequing Account (asset increase) $5,000 and credit to Unearned Revenue (liability increase) $5,000. Balance verified (debits = credits). No revenue recognized because performance obligation not yet satisfied. Tax treatment correct: deposits/prepayments are not taxable events; HST will be recognized when services are performed and revenue is earned. Amounts match transaction text. All accounts and directionality appropriate.
